trip_window: April 22–29, 2026, spring tulip trip for two couples
source_mix: friend texts (Dana, Priya), Eater Amsterdam, ChatGPT, r/AmsterdamTravel, TikTok saves, one NYT 36 Hours clipping
paste_text:
- Anne Frank House — book exactly 6 weeks out when tickets drop, no walk-up
- Van Gogh Museum — timed entry, book morning slot, closed? check dates

- Rembrandt House Museum — near Waterlooplein, book if rainy
- Canal cruise — Those Dam Boat Guys or open boat, book sunset if weather
- Jordaan wander — Nine Streets, canals, brown cafes
- Foodhallen — group dinner backup, De Hallen

- Gartine — breakfast / high tea, book ahead
- Vleminckx fries — sauce war, quick snack
- Brouwerij 't IJ — windmill brewery, afternoon beers
- Brown cafe: Cafe Chris — Jordaan, old-school, cash?
- A'DAM Lookout — swing maybe cheesy, ferry from Centraal
- NDSM Wharf — ferry, street art, Pllek for drinks
- Begijnhof — quiet courtyard, quick stop
- Vondelpark — bike ride if weather
- Bloemenmarkt — touristy flower market, quick look
- King's Day — April 27, citywide crowds/canals, wear orange, most museums may have weird hours
- Day trip: Keukenhof — open mid-March to mid-May, timed entrance + bus combo
- Keukenhof Gardens — tulips, book tickets, go early
- Haarlem — possible half-day, Frans Hals Museum, 15 min train
- Zaanse Schans — windmills, very touristy but Priya wants photos
- https://maps.app.goo.gl/Ra2kUD2y2D5vQBz99 — Dana's saved Indonesian rijsttafel place, maybe Restaurant Blauw?
- ChatGPT day 5: morning at Noord ferry + A'DAM Lookout, lunch at Pllek, afternoon NDSM street art, dinner back in Jordaan
- r/AmsterdamTravel: Moeders — Dutch comfort food, book weeks ahead
- TikTok save: Van Stapele Koekmakerij — cookie line, maybe overrated
- Anne Frank museum — duplicate from Dana's note
- Possibly closed: De Kas private dining greenhouse tour — check whether restaurant-only service is open

trip_window: Mid-September 2026, 5 days, anniversary trip for a couple in their 40s
source_mix: friend texts (Maria, Jess), Eater Barcelona, ChatGPT, r/Barcelona, TikTok saves, the Lonely Planet Spain chapter
paste_text:
- La Sagrada Família — book timed entry + tower access (Nativity tower for the older, Passion tower for the elevator)
- Park Güell — Monumental Zone is timed-entry, book 7+ days out, NOT the free park area
- Casa Batlló — book "Gaudí Premium" with the audioguide, evening visits add the music
- La Pedrera (Casa Milà) — book "Night Experience" rooftop with the projection show
- Picasso Museum — El Born, book online, free first Sunday and Thursday evenings after 5
- Wander the Gothic Quarter — early morning, get lost; Plaça Sant Felip Neri
- El Born neighborhood — evening tapas, Passeig del Born
- Gràcia — Plaça del Sol, vermouth bars, Festa Major in August (we'll miss it)
- Eixample — Passeig de Gràcia for Modernisme architecture (Block of Discord)
- La Boqueria Market — closed Sundays, El Pinotxo bar for breakfast (Juanito's stool)
- Mercat de Sant Antoni — less touristy alternative, Sunday book + coin market in the morning
- Cal Pep — tapas counter, walk-in 1:30pm sharp (closed Sun/Mon), the seafood plate
- Quimet & Quimet — tiny standing-only tapas, walk-in, the montaditos
- Bar Cañete — El Raval, book a week ahead, the chef's counter
- Disfrutar — 3-star Michelin, Eixample, book 6 months out (no joke, the menu is the spinoff from elBulli)
- El Xampanyet — cava bar El Born, walk-in (closed Mondays), anchovies + cava
- Granja M. Viader — chocolate con churros near La Boqueria, oldest dairy in Barcelona
- Federal Café — Sant Antoni branch, Australian-style brunch
- Bar Mut — Eixample, natural wine bar, walk-in (squeeze in at the counter)
- Day trip: Montserrat — train from Plaça Espanya, half-day, the boys' choir at 1pm if a school day
- Day trip: Sitges — beach town, 30 min by train from Passeig de Gràcia
- Day trip: Girona — Game of Thrones filming spots, full day by AVE high-speed
- Tibidabo — funicular up, view of the entire city plus the small amusement park at the top
- Bunkers del Carmel — sunset viewpoint, free, BYO beer and snacks, 30-min walk uphill from the metro
- Camp Nou — under renovation through 2026, tours likely suspended (check status before going)
- Beach — Barceloneta is the famous one but Bogatell is calmer and the same sand
- Flamenco at Tablao Cordobés — touristy but a real flamenco intro, book the dinner + show combo
- https://maps.app.goo.gl/HrTk7DfLpYnX9JdQ4 — Maria's Gràcia vermut pin (probably Bodega Marín or Quimet)
- ChatGPT day 4: morning Hospital de Sant Pau (more underrated than Casa Batlló), lunch at Bar Brutal (El Born), afternoon walk Parc del Laberint d'Horta
- r/Barcelona: Brunch & Cake — multiple locations, expect queues, the carrot cake
- From Jess: "the pink-awning café near the Born that has the BEST carrot cake — Brunch & Cake?"
- Park Guell — same as Park Güell above, the accents tripped Jess up (dedupe candidate)
- Possibly closed: Camp Nou (already flagged above) and Bunkers del Carmel summit (sometimes shut at sunset for crowd control)
provenance: Curated ValidaTrip sample itinerary corpus; no ValidaTrip user data; no statistical claims.

- Westminster Abbey — book online, closed to tourists Sundays (services only)

- Borough Market — Fri/Sat (Mon-Thu reduced), lunch crowds peak 12–2, Bread Ahead doughnuts
- Camden Market — Sundays, food court at the lock, the rest is mostly tourist tat
- Notting Hill — Portobello Market Saturday (antiques Fri-Sat), Books for Cooks bookshop
- Shoreditch street art walking tour — Brick Lane area, Sundays for the markets too
- Sky Garden — FREE but book 3 weeks ahead, Fenchurch Street, sunset is the move
- Dishoom Covent Garden — no reservations weekends, queue OR go before 5pm OR breakfast bacon naan
- St. JOHN — Smithfield, Fergus Henderson, nose-to-tail, book ahead, the bone marrow + parsley salad
- Padella — Borough Market area, no reservations, queue 30+ min, the pici cacio e pepe
- The Wolseley — Piccadilly, afternoon tea OR breakfast (the kippers), book ahead
- Hawksmoor Seven Dials — steaks, book ahead, the Sunday roast at any branch is a separate move
- Tayyabs — Whitechapel, lamb chops, walk-in only (no reservations), BYOB
- Hyde Park — Serpentine Galleries are free, Speakers' Corner Sunday morning
- Kew Gardens — half-day, tube to Kew Gardens station, the Palm House
- Hampton Court Palace — day trip, train from Waterloo, the maze
- Day trip: Bath — 1h15 by train from Paddington, book the Roman Baths timed entry, Sally Lunn's buns
- Day trip: Oxford — 1h train from Marylebone, college walking tour, Bodleian Library tour
- Day trip: Cotswolds — Bourton-on-the-Water + Stow-on-the-Wold (rent a car or do a Mad Max Tours day)
- A West End show — Les Misérables at the Sondheim Theatre, book ahead, balcony cheap seats fine
- Shakespeare's Globe — summer outdoor performances, £5 standing "groundling" tickets
- Daunt Books Marylebone — the Edwardian travel bookshop, just go for the photos
- https://maps.app.goo.gl/4XmL2bYqRfTpKn9D8 — Sarah's Soho cocktail bar pin (she thinks it's Swift on Old Compton St, ground floor not the basement)
- ChatGPT day 5: morning at V&A (free), lunch at Comptoir Libanais in Kensington, afternoon at Natural History Museum, evening pub crawl in Belgravia
- r/london: Maltby Street Market — Saturdays only, Bermondsey, "Borough Market without the tourists, get the gin smoothie"

trip_window: October 2026, 5 days, 10th anniversary

paste_text:

- Sainte-Chapelle — timed entry, go morning for the stained glass
- Versailles — closed Mondays, book combo with Trianon + gardens; full day, RER C
- Arc de Triomphe rooftop — separate ticket, underpass entry (don't cross the Étoile)
- Catacombs of Paris — book skip-the-line a week ahead, regularly sells out
- Sacré-Cœur + wander Montmartre afternoon (Place du Tertre is a tourist trap, skip)
- Wander Le Marais — Jess's shopping list: Merci, Ofr., Empreintes, Sézane mothership
- Jardin du Luxembourg — picnic from the rue Mouffetard market
- Musée National Picasso (Le Marais)
- Frenchie Bar à Vins — Eater Paris top wine bar, walk-in 6:30pm, 5/6 rue du Nil
- Septime — reservation EXACTLY 21 days out at midnight Paris time, 11th
- Le Comptoir du Relais — Saint-Germain, Yves Camdeborde, dinner books open 21 days out
- Breizh Café — galettes, multiple locations, Marais original is the move
- Pierre Hermé Bonaparte — macarons (Ispahan, Mogador), closed Mondays
- Du Pain et des Idées — Canal Saint-Martin, closed weekends, escargot pistache
- Café de Flore — touristy but the morning coffee + croissant is the move (avoid lunch)
- Marché des Enfants Rouges — lunch stalls, closed Mondays
- Père Lachaise — Jim Morrison, Oscar Wilde, Édith Piaf; bring the printed map from the gate
- Berthillon — Île Saint-Louis, ice cream, closed Mon/Tue
- Le Mary Celeste — cocktails, Marais, walk-in
- Day trip: Giverny — Monet's gardens, open April through November only, train from Saint-Lazare to Vernon + shuttle
- https://maps.app.goo.gl/9XqK8DfPaCGmW2nQ8 — Sophie's pin (some natural-wine bar in the 11th she swears by)
- From Mia: "the tiny pink-awning natural-wine bar on rue de Lappe, can't remember the name, near the Bastille"
- ChatGPT day 3: morning at Musée Rodin (skip the line online), lunch at Café Constant rue Saint-Dominique, afternoon at Les Invalides for Napoleon's tomb
- r/ParisTravel rec: Robert — sandwich shop on rue Saint-Maur (lines after 12:30, go at 11:45)
- TikTok save: Sébastien Gaudard pâtisserie on rue des Martyrs, the millefeuille
- Eiffel Tower at sunset for photos — Trocadéro side, NOT going up (this one's separate from the summit booking above)
- Comédie-Française — Molière if anything's on, book online
- Possibly closed: Maxim's de Paris main dining room — hours have been flaky, check before going

trip_window: April 2026, 6 days, 40th birthday with a group of 5
source_mix: friend texts (Marco, Sara, Adam), Eater Rome, ChatGPT, r/Italytravel, TikTok saves, the Lonely Planet Italy chapter
paste_text:
- Vatican Museums + Sistine Chapel — book "Skip the Line" + early access (8am opening); 3 hrs minimum
- St. Peter's Basilica — free entry, dome climb is a separate ticket (book that too, 551 steps)
- Colosseum — book the combo ticket (Roman Forum + Palatine Hill, valid 24 hrs)
- Galleria Borghese — timed entry every 2 hrs, book EXACTLY 30 days out, 2-hr visit slot
- Pantheon — entry is now ticketed (€5), free Sundays + EU residents under 25
- Trevi Fountain — early morning (7am) or late night (after 11) to skip the crowd
- Spanish Steps — sit on them = €400 fine, just walk by
- Piazza Navona — coffee at a side café, NOT the main piazza-facing ones
- Wander Trastevere evenings — Marco's list of 4 bars: Freni e Frizioni, Bar San Calisto, Pimm's Good, Niji Roma
- Monti — Via del Boschetto for vintage shopping, aperitivo at Ai Tre Scalini
- Roscioli — Salumeria con Cucina, reservations 30+ days out, the cacio e pepe is the order
- Pizzarium Bonci — Prati, walk-in, slice pizza al taglio, near the Vatican
- Da Enzo al 29 — Trastevere, no reservations, arrive 7pm sharp or queue 90 min
- Bonci Pizzarium — same as Pizzarium Bonci above (Sara wrote it twice in her text)
- Sant'Eustachio Il Caffè — espresso near Pantheon, ask for it "senza zucchero" if you don't want it pre-sweetened
- Gelateria del Teatro — three locations, the one near Via dei Coronari is best
- Trapizzino — Trastevere and Testaccio branches, the chicken cacciatora one
- Tonnarello — Trastevere, walk-in, expect a queue, the cacio e pepe is the move
- Pierluigi — fish/seafood, Piazza de' Ricci near Campo de' Fiori, book ahead, splurge dinner
- Day trip: Tivoli — Villa d'Este (fountains) + Hadrian's Villa, full day, train from Tiburtina + bus
- Castel Sant'Angelo — book online, rooftop has the Vatican view
- Catacombs of San Callisto — closed Wednesdays, on the Appian Way
- Borghese Gardens — picnic from Eataly or a market
- https://maps.app.goo.gl/RnT8Dq2MwBfXKuyL7 — Adam's rooftop bar pin in Trastevere ("the one with the view of Gianicolo, doesn't remember the name")
- ChatGPT day 4: Aventine Keyhole at sunrise, Giardino degli Aranci, Roseto Comunale, lunch at Flavio al Velavevodetto in Testaccio, Non-Catholic Cemetery (Keats and Shelley graves)
- r/Italytravel rec: Armando al Pantheon — book 90+ days out for the carbonara, two seatings only
- From Sara: "the carbonara place near the Jewish Ghetto with the green awning — Nonna Betta? Sora Margherita? something like that"
- Eater Rome: Salumeria Roscioli (same as the Roscioli above — dedupe)
- TikTok save: I Supplì on Via di San Francesco a Ripa, the cacio e pepe supplì
- Possibly closed: Domus Aurea — open weekends only, advance booking required, sometimes shut for restoration
